goal: Pivot the French audiobook tool to a React app experience for turning French text into generated audiobook MP3 files, while keeping secret-bearing audio generation on a server-side boundary.
non_goals: Continuing the previous static local web page plan as the main product direction
Exposing the ElevenLabs API key to browser code
Hosted OneDrive upload via Microsoft Graph in the first React iteration
Voice marketplace browsing or AI lesson generation in the first React iteration
constraints: Treat the previous local static web app work as superseded by the React direction.
Never expose or return the ElevenLabs API key.
Keep audio generation and filesystem writes behind server-side code.
Use environment variables for secrets and configurable defaults.
Keep the first React implementation small, reviewable, and easy to roll back.
Prefer behavior-focused tests for generation, API contracts, and core React user flows.
architecture_notes: The React app should own the interactive browser experience: text entry, title/voice/model controls, generation state, preview, download, and errors.
The backend boundary should continue to own validation, ElevenLabs request shape, filename sanitization, and direct MP3 response streaming; local file writing remains a legacy helper path.

The project may either keep the current Python generation modules behind an HTTP API or wrap them from a React-focused dev stack, but browser code must never call ElevenLabs directly with a secret key.
Vercel deployment remains a future architecture task unless storage is redesigned away from direct local OneDrive writes.
Vercel production should publish the Vite `dist` directory and handle `/api/config` plus `/api/generate` through Python Serverless Functions.
Generated MP3s should stream directly from the generation response unless durable object storage is explicitly added later.

tasks.title: Realign project docs for React app pivot
tasks.description: Update planning and progress docs to record that the previous local static web app direction is superseded and that the next product direction is a React app.
acceptance_criteria: PRD goal names the React app direction.
PRD constraints explicitly protect API secrets from browser code.
PRD tasks define the next implementation steps without treating superseded work as complete.
Progress log records the plan change and current repository state.
tasks.notes: Documentation-only task created after the rollback/change-of-plan request.

tasks.title: Choose React app stack and scaffold shell
tasks.description: Pick the React project structure that best fits this repository, then add the initial app shell with the main audiobook generation workflow visible on the first screen.
acceptance_criteria: React app can be started locally with documented commands.
First screen contains the usable audiobook generation form, not a marketing landing page.
The UI includes text, title, voice/model, voice tuning, pause, and speed controls.
The app has clear loading, success, empty, and error states.
No browser bundle contains secret environment variables.
test_plan: Run the React test suite or configured component tests.
Verify the local app in a browser.
tasks.notes: Selected Vite + React + TypeScript. Source, tests, and docs are scaffolded. Node/npm are now available and frontend tests/build pass; browser dev-server verification remains pending.

tasks.title: Connect React UI to server-side audiobook generation
tasks.description: Wire the React workflow to a server-side endpoint that uses the existing generation behavior without leaking secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting the React form calls a server-side generation endpoint.
Successful generation returns saved path metadata, segment count, preview URL, and download URL.
Preview and download URLs serve only generated files from the configured output directory.
API and UI errors are understandable and do not expose secret values.
Existing generator behavior remains covered by tests.
test_plan: Manually verify a local generation flow with mocked or configured ElevenLabs access.
tasks.notes: First-version hookup is implemented: React posts to the Python API, the API returns preview/download metadata, downloads are constrained to the output directory, and config readiness is exposed without leaking the ElevenLabs key. Frontend tests/build now pass. Blocked from done because pytest is unavailable in the Python environment and real end-to-end audio generation needs a local ElevenLabs key plus output directory.

tasks.title: Restore git repository and commit current work
tasks.description: Initialize git for this project folder, configure the required author identity, add a conventional ignore file, and commit the current tracked project state so future work can follow the commit workflow.
acceptance_criteria: The project folder is a valid git repository.
Repository-local git author identity matches the AGENTS.md instruction.
A first commit exists containing the current project files and tracking docs.
Push status is recorded clearly when no remote is configured.
test_plan: Run git status to confirm repository health.
Run git log to confirm the first commit exists.
tasks.notes: Initialized the repository on main. No remote URL was discoverable, so push remains blocked until origin is configured.

tasks.title: Connect repository to GitHub remote
tasks.description: Configure the local git repository to use the GitHub repository at tobiolabode/french_audiobook, push the current branch, and set upstream tracking.
acceptance_criteria: origin points to https://github.com/tobiolabode/french_audiobook.git.
The current main branch tracks origin/main.
Committed local work is pushed to the GitHub repository.
Progress log records the remote connection.
test_plan: Run git remote -v to confirm origin URL.
Run git branch -vv to confirm upstream tracking.
Run git status --short --branch to confirm the branch is clean and synced.
tasks.notes: origin is configured for the GitHub repository and main tracks origin/main.

tasks.title: Provision local Node and npm toolchain
tasks.description: Make Node.js and npm available locally so the React app can install dependencies, run tests, and build.
acceptance_criteria: A local `node --version` command succeeds.
A local `npm --version` command succeeds.
Frontend dependencies can be installed for this project.
`npm test` and `npm run build` have been attempted and results are recorded.
tasks.notes: Installed Node.js v22.12.0 under the ignored `.tools/nodejs` directory, added node/npm/npx shims to `C:\Users\tnint\tools`, installed frontend dependencies, generated package-lock.json, and verified npm test/build.

tasks.title: Make npm scripts reliable from Windows PowerShell UNC workspaces
tasks.description: Fix the Windows command path so TypeScript npm scripts can run from the shared WSL UNC workspace without cmd.exe changing the working directory to C:\Windows.
acceptance_criteria: `npm --version` succeeds from the UNC workspace.
`npx --version` succeeds from the UNC workspace.
`npm test` succeeds from the UNC workspace.
`npm run build` succeeds from the UNC workspace.
The package scripts avoid Windows .cmd bin shims for TypeScript tooling.
tasks.notes: PowerShell resolves npm/npx through executable shims in `C:\Users\tnint\tools`; UNC WSL workspaces are routed to the repo-local Linux Node v22.12.0 under ignored `.tools/`, and package scripts invoke TypeScript tooling through Node entrypoints.

tasks.title: Prepare app for Vercel web deployment
tasks.description: Refactor the React and Python app shape so Vercel can publish the frontend as a static Vite app and run generation through serverless Python API functions without local persistent audio storage.
acceptance_criteria: Vite builds to the standard `dist` output directory used by Vercel.
`vercel.json` defines the build output and API routing needed for a Vite SPA with Python functions.
`/api/config` and `/api/generate` exist as Vercel function entrypoints.
`/api/generate` returns the generated MP3 directly instead of writing to or serving from a durable local directory.
The React app previews and downloads generated audio from a browser object URL.
Vercel deployment requirements are documented.
The untracked `.venv/` directory is ignored.
test_plan: Run backend tests covering storage-free generation and API response helpers.
Run frontend tests covering streamed MP3 preview and download.
Run Python compile checks for source, tests, and API functions.
tasks.notes: Created from the Vercel audit findings: persistent Python HTTP server, dev-proxy-only API routes, local MP3 storage, nonstandard build output, missing Vercel docs/config, and unignored `.venv/`.

tasks.title: Deploy new Vercel project
tasks.description: Create or link a new Vercel project for this repository and deploy the current Vercel-ready web app.
acceptance_criteria: A Vercel deployment is created from the current repository state.
The deployment reaches READY status or the failure is diagnosed from deployment details/logs.
The deployment URL is recorded in progress notes.
Any required runtime environment variable gaps are documented.
Tracking files are updated, committed, and pushed.
test_plan: Use the Vercel connector or CLI to deploy.
Fetch the deployed URL or otherwise verify the deployment is reachable.
Run local build/tests if deployment requires code changes.
tasks.notes: New Vercel project `french_audiobook` was created and production deployment `dpl_GLXqZFNN6feYCAFHMae6xaL2Lp9J` reached READY. First CLI deploy linked the project but failed before build because local toolchain folders exceeded Vercel's 100 MB file upload limit; `.vercelignore` fixed the upload. Runtime generation still requires adding `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` in Vercel.

tasks.title: Add ElevenLabs API key to Vercel production
tasks.description: Store the ElevenLabs API key in Vercel's encrypted production environment variables and redeploy so the live API can generate audio.
acceptance_criteria: `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` is added to the Vercel production environment without committing the secret.
Production is redeployed after the environment variable is added.
`/api/config` no longer reports `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` as missing.
Progress notes record the deployment ID and verification results without storing the secret value.
tasks.notes: Production deployment `dpl_F86FQPhjbqHS7CuzK8G4Kcbp7qvD` reached READY after adding the encrypted Vercel environment variable. The secret value is intentionally not recorded in repository files.

tasks.title: Fix Generate MP3 button state and add generation logs
tasks.description: Make the Generate MP3 button usable when the app only needs a Voice ID, remove the misleading loading cursor from non-busy disabled states, and add safe client/server logs for generation debugging.
acceptance_criteria: The Generate MP3 button is enabled when server config is ready even if the user still needs to enter a required Voice ID.
Disabled buttons no longer show the loading cursor unless generation is actively running.
Generation requests cannot hang the UI indefinitely without surfacing a timeout error.
Browser console logs show config loading, generation request, response, success, and failure milestones without exposing secrets or full text.
Server logs show sanitized generation request, success, validation error, and config error details.
test_plan: Add a frontend regression test for the missing-default-voice but ready-server-config state.
tasks.notes: The screenshot showed a disabled Generate MP3 button while `has_default_voice` was false. The UI now leaves the button enabled when only Voice ID input is missing so native required-field validation can guide the user. Implementation is committed locally; completion is blocked only on explicit approval to push to the external GitHub remote.

tasks.title: Use default voice ID and unblock MP3 download flow
tasks.description: Configure a built-in default ElevenLabs voice ID, expose it through non-secret config, and populate the React Voice ID field so users do not need to type it before generating and downloading MP3 files.
acceptance_criteria: The backend uses `JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb` as the default voice ID when `ELEVENLABS_DEFAULT_VOICE_ID` is not set.
`/api/config` includes the non-secret default voice ID and reports `has_default_voice: true` when the built-in default is active.
The React form fills the Voice ID field from config instead of leaving it empty.
The Generate MP3 button is not disabled by an empty Voice ID field when a default voice exists.
Generated MP3 output still renders a normal download link after a successful response.
Documentation shows the default voice ID and explains it can be overridden.
test_plan: Add backend tests for built-in default voice fallback and config payload.
Add frontend tests for default Voice ID autofill and download link rendering.
Verify local `/api/config` reports the default voice ID.
tasks.notes: Implementation and tests are passing locally. Completion remains blocked until the local commits can be pushed/deployed to the external GitHub/Vercel production path with explicit user approval.

completion_criteria: The React workflow can generate, preview, and download audiobook MP3s through a server-side boundary.
Relevant tests and build checks pass.
Planning docs and progress log reflect completed tasks.
Changes are committed and pushed from a valid git repository.
